content.problemStatement:
There is a container AAA filled with N blocks placed from left to right, where the size of block placed at ithi^{th}ith position from the left has size AiA_iAi​. There is another **empty** container BBB (identical as AAA) in which you wish to transfer the blocks.

To arrange the blocks you are given a vertical buffer where you can pile up the blocks.
You are allowed to perform any of the two moves multiple times -


Pick the **rightmost** block from container AAA and put it on the **top** of the buffer.
Pick the **topmost** block from the buffer and put it in the **rightmost** unoccupied space in container BBB.
Your task is to determine if it is possible to place all the blocks from container AAA to container BBB such that their size is non decreasing order. More formally, Bi≤Bi+1B_i \leq B_{i+1}Bi​≤Bi+1​ for all 1≤i<N 1 \leq i \lt N1≤i<N.


Input Format

First line will contain TTT, number of testcases. Then the testcases follow.
First line of each testcase contains an integer NNN - number of blocks in container AAA.
Second line contains N space separated integers A1,A2,.....,ANA_1, A_2, ....., A_NA1​,A2​,.....,AN​


Output Format

For each test case, output a single line containing either "Yes" if possible or "No" if not possible.
You may print each character of the string in uppercase or lowercase (for example: the strings "yes", "YeS", "YES" will be treated as the same strings).
constraints: Sum of NNN over all test cases will not exceed 10510^5105

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Explanation:
Test Case 1 : Follow the following moves

Pick rightmost block from A (4 in this case) and put in the buffer.   A = [ 2 1 3 5 4 ], buffer = [ 4 ] and B = [ ]
Pick rightmost block from A (5 in this case) and put in the buffer.    A = [ 2 1 3 ], buffer = [ 5 4 ] and B = [ ]
Pick topmost block from buffer (5 in this case) and put in B.   A = [ 2 1 3 ],  buffer = [ 4 ] and B = [ 5 ]
Pick topmost block from buffer (4 in this case) and put in B.   A = [ 2 1 3 ],  buffer = [  ] and B = [ 4 5 ]
Pick rightmost block from A (3 in this case) and put in the buffer.    A = [ 2 1 ],  buffer = [ 3 ] and B = [ 4 5 ]
Pick topmost block from buffer (3 in this case) and put in B.   A = [ 2 1 ],  buffer = [  ] and B = [ 3 4 5 ]
Pick rightmost block from A (1 in this case) and put in the buffer.    A = [ 2 ],  buffer = [ 1 ] and B = [ 3 4 5 ]
Pick rightmost block from A (2 in this case) and put in the buffer.   A = [ ],  buffer = [ 2 1 ] and B = [ 3 4 5 ]
Pick topmost block from buffer (2 in this case) and put in B.   A = [ ],  buffer = [ 1 ] and B = [ 2 3 4 5 ]
Pick topmost block from buffer (1 in this case) and put in B.   A = [ ],  buffer = [  ] and B = [ 1 2 3 4 5 ]

We can see Container B contains all blocks in increasing order of their size.
Test Case 2 : It can be proved that there exists no sequence of moves such that container B contains blocks in increasing order of their sizes.
